definition: AFLFast FAST power schedule multiplies havoc mutation budget (up to 4x via n_fuzz-based perf_score) for rarely-hit coverage buckets, giving seeds near a structural non-null/presence gate extra mutation energy to satisfy it; the opposing minimizer fuzzer (calibration-only energy, no rarity weighting) receives no such boost and blocks.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: AFLFast FAST power schedule multiplies mutation budget up to 4x for rarely-hit coverage buckets, giving seeds that partially satisfy the Host-header pointer check more havoc mutations to complete it; minimizer lacks this rarity weighting.
signature.one_line: FAST schedule boosts havoc budget on rare Host-header seeds

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must contain a literal "Host:" custom header so Curl_checkheaders returns non-NULL ptr and the true branch is taken

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same program-side requirement — a "Host:" header making ptr non-NULL — but reached purely by extra havoc/token mutation budget rather than CMP introspection, since both fast and minimizer lack I2S.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: fast resolves 8/10 vs minimizer 2/10 with hitcount 394 vs 75; fast's AFLFast FAST power schedule gives rarely-hit coverage buckets up to 4x energy, so the rare seeds that already contain a partial "Host:" header get many more havoc mutations and more often complete the literal.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: minimizer uses PowerSchedule None with energy from intrinsic calibration only, not weighted by region rarity, so the rare path to the "Host:"-bearing input gets no extra mutation budget and it blocks 8/10 with far lower hitcount (75).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The fast vs minimizer delta is purely the AFLFast `n_fuzz`-based rare-edge power schedule (FAST) which multiplies the mutation budget for rarely-exercised coverage buckets; neither fuzzer has CMP logging, so the win is extra havoc dosing on rare paths, not comparison-solving.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker true side requires ptr non-NULL, where ptr is set by Curl_checkheaders for the "Host" header.
The blocker condition gates the true branch on ptr being non-NULL.
Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal "Host:" bytes 47 48 6f 73 74 3a at offset 0x24.
Winner bytes at the header offsets are saturated to the "Host:" characters while loser bytes are scattered.
evidence_trail.cited_section: BYTE DIFF (W vs L at common offsets)

evidence_trail.claim: Loser-blocking seeds lack a clean "Host:" literal at the header offsets.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a CMP operand at matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: The winner vpc seed's ancestor chain contains I2S-floor dash rows, direct lower-bound evidence of an I2S contribution.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
their presence in a winning
seed's ancestor chain is direct (lower-bound) evidence of I2S
contribution.

evidence_trail.claim: The fast vs minimizer delta is the AFLFast rare-edge power schedule giving rare paths up to 4x energy.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Rarely-hit
paths get up to 4× energy; saturated paths are damped to 0.4×.

evidence_trail.claim: minimizer uses PowerSchedule None so energy is not weighted by region hit-frequency.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
PowerSchedule is `None`, so the
energy uses ONLY intrinsic calibration and is NOT weighted by how often
a region has been hit.

evidence_trail.claim: fast resolves more trials and has much higher branch hitcount than minimizer.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness that gates on a non-NULL Host-header lookup but whose header-name literal is supplied at runtime (not present as a constant CMP operand) showing cmplog/vpc with no advantage over naive/value_profile would refute the I2S-literal hypothesis.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Whether the fast>minimizer win is genuinely the AFLFast rarity schedule rather than noise — its subject-level p_AUC=0.1041 is not significant and prob_div is only 0.60.

signature.mechanism_summary: AFLFast FAST rare-edge power schedule concentrates havoc budget on the rarely-exercised cookie-path seed, resolving the non-null pointer check; minimizer's calibration-only energy provides no rarity-weighted boost.
signature.one_line: FAST rarity schedule amplifies havoc on rare cookie-path seeds

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must spell a Host-header literal so the cookie engine activates and the host pointer is taken at L2792.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same structural Host-header / cookie-engine input as above, but reached without CMP introspection — purely via more havoc energy budgeted to the rarely-hit seed that approaches the cookie path.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: fast uses the AFLFast FAST power schedule giving rarely-hit coverage buckets up to 4x energy, so the seed near the cookie branch receives more havoc mutations and resolves 8/10 with avg hitcount 214 on the branch.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: minimizer uses PowerSchedule None (intrinsic calibration energy only, not weighted by region rarity), so the same rare path gets ordinary budget and resolves only 2/10 with avg hitcount 53.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: fast's AFLFast rare-edge power schedule (n_fuzz-based perf_score boost) concentrates havoc budget on the rarely-exercised cookie path; minimizer's calibration-only energy does not, so fast wins by scheduling not by CMP-solving.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the host-pointer ternary at L2792, true side globally blocked.
Two pairs differ by the I2S technique: cmplog>naive and value_profile_cmplog>value_profile.
value_profile_cmplog>value_profile is also an I2S-delta decisive pair.
Winner seeds carry a concentrated Host-header literal at offsets 0x24-0x29.
Winner side offset 0x28 is t and 0x27 is s/S, spelling Host with case variation.
The true side at L2792 is taken 30 times in W and 0 times in L.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which scans the input for an operand and substitutes the other operand.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile has no I2SRandReplace mutator.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Mutators**: naive's havoc + token stack. No `I2SRandReplace`.

evidence_trail.claim: fast wins by AFLFast power schedule giving rarely-hit paths up to 4x energy.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Rarely-hit
paths get up to 4× energy; saturated paths are damped to 0.4×.

evidence_trail.claim: minimizer uses PowerSchedule None so energy is not weighted by region hit-frequency.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
PowerSchedule is `None`, so the
energy uses ONLY intrinsic calibration and is NOT weighted by how often
a region has been hit.

evidence_trail.claim: fast resolves 8/10 with avg hitcount 214 vs minimizer 2/10 with avg hitcount 53.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=214  loser=53

evidence_trail.claim: fast vs minimizer is the aflfast_rarity single-technique delta.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating L2792 on a Host-header literal where cmplog/vpc fail to resolve it while naive/value_profile resolve it at equal rates would refute the I2S attribution; for the aflfast hypothesis, fast and minimizer resolving at equal rates on a rare-path harness would refute it.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The Host-header byte interpretation rests on case-varying offsets 0x24-0x29 spelling 'Host:'; the leading 'G' at 0x24 and the proto bytes are noisy, so the exact CMP operand string is inferred rather than directly logged.
